definitions.example: Finding the equation of a line passing through two given points.
definitions.definition: The study of geometry using the coordinate system, where points are represented by their (x, y) coordinates. It allows us to solve problems involving straight lines, circles, and other geometric shapes using algebraic methods.

definitions.example: The line segment joining the points (2, 3) and (4, 5).
definitions.definition: A part of a line that has two endpoints and extends indefinitely in one direction. It is denoted as AB, where A and B are the endpoints.

definitions.example: Finding the mid-point of the line segment joining (1, 2) and (3, 4).
definitions.definition: The point that divides a line segment into two equal parts. It is the average of the coordinates of the two endpoints.

definitions.example: The x-axis or the y-axis in a coordinate plane.
definitions.definition: A set of points that extend indefinitely in two directions and have a common point (the origin) or two distinct points (the line segment).

definitions.example: Finding the slope of the line passing through the points (1, 2) and (3, 4).
definitions.definition: A measure of how steep a line is. It is calculated as the ratio of the vertical change to the horizontal change between two points on the line.

definitions.example: The gradient of the line y = 2x + 1.
definitions.definition: Another term for slope, used to describe how steep a line is.

definitions.example: Finding the equation of the line passing through the points (1, 2) and (3, 4).
definitions.definition: A mathematical expression that describes the relationship between the x and y coordinates of points on a line. It is typically in the form y = mx + c, where m is the slope and c is the y-intercept.

definitions.example: Finding the y-intercept of the line y = 2x + 1.
definitions.definition: The point at which a line crosses the y-axis. It is the value of y when x is 0.

definitions.example: Finding the x-intercept of the line y = 2x + 1.
definitions.definition: The point at which a line crosses the x-axis. It is the value of x when y is 0.

definitions.example: The x-axis and the line y = 2x + 1.
definitions.definition: Lines that lie in the same plane and never intersect, no matter how far they are extended.

definitions.example: The x-axis and the line y = -x.
definitions.definition: Lines that intersect at a right angle (90 degrees).

definitions.example: Finding the equation of the line passing through the point (1, 2) and having a slope of 3.
definitions.definition: A way of writing the equation of a line using the slope and a point on the line. It is in the form y - y1 = m(x - x1), where (x1, y1) is the point and m is the slope.

definitions.example: Finding the equation of the line passing through the points (1, 2) and (3, 4).
definitions.definition: A way of writing the equation of a line using two points on the line. It is in the form y - y1 = m(x - x1), where (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) are the two points.

definitions.example: Finding the normal to the line y = 2x + 1.
definitions.definition: A line that is perpendicular to a given line.

definitions.example: Finding the tangent to the curve y = x^2 at the point (1, 1).
definitions.definition: A line that touches a curve at a single point. It has the same slope as the curve at that point.

definitions.term: Distance between a Point and a Line
definitions.example: Finding the distance between the point (1, 2) and the line y = 2x + 1.
definitions.definition: The shortest distance between a point and a line. It is calculated using the formula d = |Ax + By + C| / sqrt(A^2 + B^2), where A, B, and C are the coefficients of the line and (x, y) is the point.

definitions.term: Line passing through a given point and having a given slope
definitions.example: Finding the equation of the line passing through the point (1, 2) and having a slope of 3.
definitions.definition: A line that passes through a given point and has a given slope. Its equation can be found using the point-slope form.

definitions.term: Equation of a line in the form Ax + By + C = 0
definitions.example: Finding the equation of the line passing through the points (1, 2) and (3, 4) in the form Ax + By + C = 0.
definitions.definition: A form of the equation of a line where the coefficients A, B, and C are constants.

definitions.example: Finding the general equation of a line that passes through the origin.
definitions.definition: An equation that describes all lines in a coordinate plane. It is in the form Ax + By + C = 0, where A, B, and C are constants.

definitions.example: Finding the angle between the lines y = 2x + 1 and y = x.
definitions.definition: The angle between the two lines. It is calculated using the formula tanθ = |m1 - m2| / (1 + m1m2), where m1 and m2 are the slopes of the two lines.
